steps.title: Convert the percentage increase to a growth multiplier
steps.content: A 158% increase means the new mass is the old mass plus 158% of the old mass. This is equivalent to multiplying the old mass by $1 + 1.58 = 2.58$. So the mass multiplies by 2.58 at the end of every 5-week period.

steps.title: Write the general exponential model
steps.content:
After $t$ weeks, the number of complete 5-week periods is $\frac{t}{5}$. Starting from an initial mass $M_0$, after each period the mass is multiplied by 2.58, so after $\frac{t}{5}$ periods:
$$M = M_0 \cdot 2.58^{\frac{t}{5}}$$
Note: the exponent is $\frac{t}{5}$ (not $\frac{5}{t}$), so as $t$ increases, the exponent increases and $M$ grows.

steps.title: Solve for the initial mass using the data point
steps.content:
At $t = 15$ weeks, we know $M = 627.7$ grams. Substitute into the model:
$$627.7 = M_0 \cdot 2.58^{\frac{15}{5}} = M_0 \cdot 2.58^3$$
Compute $2.58^3 \approx 17.17$. Then:
$$M_0 = \frac{627.7}{17.17} \approx 36.55$$
So the equation is $M = 36.55 \cdot 2.58^{\frac{t}{5}}$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
